creator: grog@lemis.com
text:
Update to /usr/ports/mail/p5-Mail-SpamAssassin/pkg-message

Fix: 

--- pkg-message	15 Jun 2008 11:59:20 +1000	1.7
+++ pkg-message	16 Jun 2009 11:54:48 +1000	
@@ -6,7 +6,7 @@
 *	/_/   \_\_|   |_| |_____|_| \_| |_| |___\___/|_| \_|		*
 *									*
 *	See PREFIX/share/doc/p5-Mail-SpamAssassin/INSTALL,		*
-*	and PREFIX/share/doc/p5-Mail-SpamAssassin/UPGRADE,		*
+*	PREFIX/share/doc/p5-Mail-SpamAssassin/UPGRADE 		        *
 *	or http://spamassassin.org/dist/INSTALL and			*
 *	http://spamassassin.org/dist/UPGRADE BEFORE enabling		*
 *	this version of SpamAssassin for important information		*
@@ -21,10 +21,10 @@
 *************************************************************************
 You may wish to run sa-update now to obtain the latest rules.
 
-NOTE:  FREEBSD users: If you are updating to 3.20 you will find
+NOTE:  FreeBSD users: If you are updating to 3.20 you will find
 that sa-update now places state files in /var/db/spamassassin
-and not the default /var/lib/spamassassin.  This is to be consistant
-with Freebsd file directory conventions
+and not the default /var/lib/spamassassin.  This is to be consistent
+with FreeBSD file directory conventions.
 
 If you run sa-compile, you will notice that files are in 
 /var/db/spamassassin/compiled/<perlversion>/<version> instead of
@@ -32,8 +32,10 @@
 No attempts have been made to move old versions over. You must recompile.
 
 If you are running with spamd, you must add the following to rc.conf:
-spamd_enable="YES"
+spamd_enable="YES".  Note that the startup script is called
+/usr/local/etc/rc.d/sa-spamd.sh, not /usr/local/etc/rc.d/spamd.sh.
+
+Security Note: If you did NOT deselect AS_ROOT, spamd will run as
+root.  To change this, also add this to rc.conf:
 
-Security Note:  If you did NOT deselected AS_ROOT, spamd will be running
-as root. To change this, also add this to rc.conf:
 spamd_flags="-u spamd -H /var/spool/spamd"
